chapter_title.ar: بَابُ أَنَّ الْأَئِمَّةَ ( عليهم السلام ) يَعْلَمُونَ مَتَى يَمُوتُونَ وَ أَنَّهُمْ لَا يَمُوتُونَ إِلَّا بِاخْتِيَارٍ مِنْهُمْ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 47 – The Imams<sup>asws</sup> are knowing when they<sup>asws</sup> would be passing away, and they<sup>asws</sup> are not passing away except by a choice from them<sup>asws</sup>

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/119)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/119)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) أَيُّ إِمَامٍ لَا يَعْلَمُ مَا يُصِيبُهُ وَ إِلَى مَا يَصِيرُ فَلَيْسَ ذَلِكَ بِحُجَّةٍ لِلَّهِ عَلَى خَلْقِهِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin yahya, from Salma Bin Al Khattab, from Suleyman Bin Sama’at, and Abdullah Bin Muhammad, from Abdullah Bin Al Qasim Al Batl, from Abu Baseer who said,
‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Whichever Imam does not know what is to hit him and to what he would be proceeding, so that one isn’t with a Divine Authority upon His<sup>azwj</sup> creatures’.
en.sarwar: 1. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Salamah ibn-al-Khattab from ) Sulayman ibn Sama‘a and ‘Abdallah ibn Muhammad from ‘Abdallah ibn al-Qasim al-Batal from abu Basir from abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) who has said the following. “Whoever of the Imams that would not know what would happen to him and to what events he would proceed such a person is not a possessor of Divine authority over His creature.”
